titleStmt.title: SII 9.66: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 66. (A.R. No. 100 of 1904.) ON THE 16th SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 878, Naḷa, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti corresponding probably to A.D. 956 December 23 Tuesday. It refers itself to the reign of the Rāshṭrakūṭa king Akāḷavarsha-Kannaradēva. The king's Mahāsāmanta Ṟoṭṭayya ruling over Māsivāḍi hundred-and-forty, Kōgaḷi-five-hundred and Kukkanūr thirty, made a grant of the whole of the revenue raised from Bābandi to Paśupatibhaṭṭa, the Ūroḍeya of Bāḷguḷi. Another chief named Dhōrapayya is also said to have made some gift. As the inscription is damaged, the nature of the gift cannot be made out.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastyakāḷavarṣadēva śrīpṛthvīvallabha mahārājā
dhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭārakan calakenallātaṁ
śrīmatkannaradēvana rājyamuttarōttarābhivṛddhige
saluttamire svasti samadhigatapañcamahāśa
bda mahāsamanta śaucārṇnavaṁ sāraloḷḷidōṁ sammanadā
ṇmaṁ pagegebalgaṇḍa nuḍidantegaṇḍa kṛtayugagaḷḷaṁ śrī
mat ṟoṭṭayyaṁ māsivāḍi nūṟanālvattuvaṁ kōgaḻi
yaynūṟuvaṁ kukkanūrmmūvattuvanāḷuttamire sva
sti sakanṛpakāḷātītasaṁvatsarasataṁga 878 neya
naḷasaṁvatsara pravartiseyuttarāyaṇasaṁkrāntiyūḷ
bāḷguḷiyūroḍeyaṁ paśupatibhaṭṭaṅge bā
bandiya sambandhada rājadravyamādudellavaṁ
biṭṭar Iṁtappuda Ayvadiṁbaru kādu bāḻvar .
svasti samadhigatapañcamahāśabda mahā
sāmanta saucāṁjanēya sāhasabhīma samarattri
nētraṁ mārttalesiṅgha dhaḻamoḍega(ṁ)ṇḍa pageva
raṁkusaṁ cāḷukyanārāyaṇaṁ Ēkavākyaṁ pri
yagaḷḷaṁ Arūḍhasarvvajña(ṁ)nāśritajana
kalpavṛkṣaṁ śrīmatu dhōrapayyaṁ tama
na koṭṭa dharmmavaṁ pratipāḷisido
paradattaṁ vā yō harēta vasundha
ṣṭiṁ varṣasahasraṇi viṣṭā .
yatē krimiḥ © sāmā
turnṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanī
yō bhavadbhiḥ . sarbbānētā(ṁ)nbhāginaḥ pārtthi
vēndrāḥ bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmabhadraḥ .
Digital edition of SII 9.66 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
